notes.total: 233 tickets is a strong quarter pipeline, balanced across 8 sprints with consistent throughput at 2.5 tickets/day.
notes.completed: All 233 tickets completed — 100% delivery against plan with no carryover into Q4.
notes.completion_rate: Perfect 100% completion rate demonstrates excellent sprint discipline and realistic scope planning throughout the quarter.
notes.releases_shipped: 19 releases across 8 sprints (median 2 per sprint) shows excellent deployment cadence and continuous delivery rhythm.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-sprint additions indicates disciplined sprint planning with no reactive scope changes during execution.
notes.oos_open: No open out-of-sprint items — clean quarter close with no unplanned technical debt.
notes.type_split: Healthy mix with 57% tasks, 24% stories, 19% bugs — bug ratio is acceptable and indicates stable product quality.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 2 releases per sprint (avg 2.4) reflects strong, consistent shipping cadence across all sprints.
notes.time_logged: Only 384 hours logged (17% of estimated 2,321 hours) — severe time tracking gap makes capacity planning unreliable.
notes.estimate_accuracy: 83% variance (17% accuracy) indicates estimates are dramatically inflated or time logging is critically incomplete.
notes.no_estimate: 39% of tickets (90 items) had no estimate — planning data is unreliable and should be mandatory for future quarters.
notes.rollover: Zero rollover — all sprint commitments were met on time with no debt carried between sprints.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 13 days is acceptable, though mean of 18.5 days suggests some tickets experienced delays or blockers.
notes.assignee_workload: Ivor Didovic carried 31% of the load (72 tickets, 332 hours logged) — workload is concentrated but manageable; most team members did not log time despite completing work.
